name: Breakfast Potato & Egg Wrap
method: Grate the peeled potato using the large holes of a grater.
Squeeze out as much water as possible using kitchen paper or a clean towel.
Mix potato with salt, pepper, garlic powder, smoked paprika and Frylight.
Air fry at 200°C for 15–18 minutes, shaking halfway, until crispy.
Scramble eggs over low to medium-low heat for 2–3 minutes until just set.
Warm the wrap, spread nacho cheese sauce, add potato, eggs and ketchup.
Fold tightly and toast in a dry pan for about 2 minutes per side.

name: Protein Yogurt Bowl
method: Mix yogurt and protein powder until smooth.
Add banana and blueberries.
Crush or serve with rice cakes for crunch.

name: Lean Beef Burger Wrap
method: Cook mince in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat for 5–6 minutes.
Add seasoning once mostly browned, then cook another 2–3 minutes until no pink remains.
Warm the wrap, add lettuce, beef, avocado, burger sauce and mayo.
Fold tightly and toast in a dry pan for 1–2 minutes per side if desired.

name: Lean High-Protein Double Cheeseburger
method: Divide 150 g beef into two loose 75 g balls without kneading or compacting the meat.
Mix the salt, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika and black pepper together.
Heat a dry heavy or non-stick frying pan over high heat for 2–3 minutes until properly hot.
Place the beef balls in the pan. Put baking paper over each and immediately press very firmly with a sturdy spatula for about 10 seconds, forming thin 8–10 mm patties.
Remove the paper and season the exposed sides with half of the seasoning mixture.
Cook untouched for about 2 minutes until a dark brown crust forms. Scrape firmly underneath each patty and flip.
Season the cooked sides with the remaining seasoning and cook for another 1.5–2 minutes until thoroughly cooked.
Add 10 g Eatlean grated cheese to each patty during the final 45 seconds. Add 1 teaspoon of water to an empty area of the pan and cover for 30–45 seconds to help the low-fat cheese melt.
Remove the patties. Reduce the heat to medium and toast the brioche bun cut-side down in the same pan for 45–60 seconds.

Build in this order: bottom bun, 10 g lighter-than-light mayo, 10 g burger sauce, 30 g shredded lettuce, first cheesy patty, 10 g nacho cheese sauce, second cheesy patty, 15 g zero-sugar ketchup, then the top bun. Serve immediately.

name: Crispy Bean Wrap
method: Drain most of the liquid from the beans but do not rinse.
Heat beans until hot.
Warm the tortilla for 10–15 seconds.
Add beans and nacho cheese sauce, fold tightly.
Crisp in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes per side or air fry at 190°C for 4–6 minutes.
Add lettuce after crisping if you want it fresh and crunchy.

name: Lemon Herb Salmon & Salad
method: Cook salmon according to pack instructions or until internal temperature reaches 63°C.
Serve with salad mix.
Add potato, rice or sweet potato if you need more carbs.

name: Salmon Rice Bowl
method: Heat rice safely according to pack instructions.
Add salmon, avocado, edamame and yogurt.
Season with salt, pepper, soy sauce, ketchup or cheese sauce as preferred.

name: Salted Caramel Cocoa Hot Chocolate
seasoning: Salted caramel zero-calorie sweetener to taste
method: Add cocoa powder, Marvel and a tiny pinch of salt to a mug.
Add 1–2 tbsp boiling water and stir into a smooth paste.
Add sweetener to taste.
Slowly add 250–300 ml hot water while stirring.

name: Nescafé Gold with Marvel & Sweetener
method: Mix Marvel with a small splash of cold water to form a paste.
Add Nescafé Gold and sweetener.
Top with hot water while stirring.
